title: RPYR-0006 — `collections.deque(...) * n` aborts on a capacity overflow: `_mul` is missing the memory-size guard every other sequence has (`_collections.rs:317`)
one_line_repro: import sys; from collections import deque; deque([0]) * sys.maxsize
toolkit_class: arithmetic-overflow (pattern-set gap — abort inside std's allocator)
guarded_twin: crates/vm/src/sequence.rs — SequenceMutExt::imul / SequenceExt::mul apply the `n > 1 && size_of_val >= MAX_MEMORY_SIZE / n` guard; list/tuple/str/bytes/array all route through it. deque hand-rolls _mul and only calls the isize-overflow guard.
prior_art: unreported (the SIBLING fix is RustPython PR #8270, merged 4 days before the reviewed commit)
notes: SCANNER-BLIND, HISTORY-FOUND. deque._mul only calls check_repeat_or_overflow_error(len, n) (the isize guard); for an unbounded deque a small len with a huge n passes that check, then `deque.iter().cycle().take(mul_len).collect::<VecDeque>()` pre-allocates from the size-hint and mul_len * size_of::<PyObjectRef>() overflows isize::MAX -> VecDeque capacity-overflow panic. Seed fix: bb49898b7 'Fix overflow handling for inplace sequence repetition (#8270)' added the size guard to SequenceMutExt::imul; deque never propagated it. No test guards it.
